meta_title: For Students: debugging with AI to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debug with AI: The Student's Guide to Building a Portfolio
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with empty portfolios. Learn to master debugging with AI using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship your first production-ready SaaS project.
content.answer_block: Transform your coding workflow by using AI to handle complex debugging. By leveraging Antigravity for architectural oversight and Claude Code for rapid implementation, you can ship complex projects faster. This process turns error-prone learning into high-quality portfolio pieces, proving your ability to solve real-world problems to future employers.
content.intro:
The screen glows, the cursor blinks, and your project remains stuck in a cycle of cryptic errors. For a student, the gap between 'learning to code' and 'shipping a product' feels like an abyss. You have the ambition, but the lack of a tangible portfolio is the silent killer of your career prospects.

But the rules of the game have changed. You no longer need to struggle in isolation. By integrating intelligent agents into your development lifecycle, you can leapfrog the junior stage, turning debugging from a frustration into a masterclass in software architecture. Let's build your first project.

steps.title: Architectural Scoping
steps.description: Define the scope to avoid feature creep. Use AI to map out your MVP requirements and identify potential technical bottlenecks before writing a single line of code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Architect. Review this project scope [Insert Scope] and identify the top three risks that could cause a deployment failure for a beginner.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --architect 'Create a technical roadmap for this project'.

steps.title: Rapid Prototyping
steps.description: Generate the foundational boilerplate. Focus on clean architecture so your code remains maintainable and impressive to recruiters.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready boilerplate for [Project Name] using [Tech Stack]. Emphasize modular design and error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --generate 'Setup project structure for a standard MVC architecture'.

steps.title: Intelligent Debugging
steps.description: When errors occur, don't just copy-paste. Use AI to explain the root cause, teaching you the underlying mechanics of the framework.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Explain this stack trace [Insert Error] as if I am a junior developer. What is the root cause and how do I prevent this pattern?

steps.title: Refactoring for Performance
steps.description: Clean up your code to show professional rigor. Review your logic with AI to ensure it follows industry-standard design patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review this function [Insert Code] for efficiency and readability. Suggest improvements based on Clean Code principles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --refactor 'Optimize the current function for O(n) complexity'.

steps.title: Deployment & Documentation
steps.description: A project without a README is invisible. Automate the documentation to ensure your portfolio piece looks professional.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a professional README.md for this repository that highlights the problem solved, the tech stack, and my specific contributions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --docs 'Generate a comprehensive README for this repository'.

pro_tips: Treat AI as a pair programmer, not a shortcut; always verify the logic before merging.
Document your debugging process; recruiters value a candidate who can explain how they solved a complex bug.
Keep your context window clean by summarizing progress every 50 lines of code.
Use AI to generate unit tests; a project with 80% test coverage stands out significantly in a junior portfolio.
Build in public by sharing the 'before and after' of your AI-assisted refactoring on LinkedIn.
content.next_steps: Once your first project is live, deploy it to Vercel or Railway, document the repository, and iterate on one new feature per week to demonstrate consistency.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped with the tools to turn your coding struggles into a high-impact portfolio. Don't let your code sit on your local machine gathering digital dust. Ship it, show it off, and get hired. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
